name: Honey (Corporate Social Network)

quality.reason: Enterprise social network competing with Slack

clarity.reason: Corporate social network value prop unclear vs Slack

differentiation.reason: Weak differentiation in crowded enterprise space

retention_design.reason: Network effects needed for enterprise social

diagnosis.main_signal: Final PH launch shows near-zero interest

failure_reasons: Repeated PH launches declining in engagement
notes: Classic low_exposure_death pattern. Three PH launches with declining engagement (6->3->1 votes). Without independent distribution, repeated launches are just noise. Enterprise social network market was already dominated by Slack.

name: Wappalyzer

differentiation.reason: Only comprehensive tech stack detection tool

pain_intensity.reason: Developers need tech stack info for competitive analysis

diagnosis.main_signal: PH zero interest but GitHub success

success_reasons: GitHub and Chrome Web Store as effective channels
Technical differentiation in tech stack detection
failure_reasons: ProductHunt completely wrong channel for developer tools
General product launch platforms ineffective for dev tools
key_events: Ongoing GitHub and Chrome Web Store success
notes: Wappalyzer is a textbook case of channel-product mismatch. The product thrives on GitHub and Chrome Web Store but gets zero traction on ProductHunt. Developer tools need developer channels (GitHub, HN, Dev.to), not general product launch platforms.

name: Arc Browser
target_user: Power users, knowledge workers, early adopters

retention_design.reason: Spaces, profiles, and workflows create stickiness

notes: Arc Browser represents the innovation_premium pattern. Loved by users (4.8 stars, 238 reviews) but could not overcome Chrome's ecosystem moat. Acquisition by Atlassian may give it new life but at the cost of independence.

name: vidIQ

diagnosis.main_signal: Low PH but strong Chrome adoption

failure_reasons: PH wrong channel for developer tools
Feature war with TubeBuddy exhausting resources
notes: vidIQ demonstrates niche_compound + content_marketing path. Despite zero PH traction (4 votes), the product thrives through Chrome Web Store and YouTube community. Content marketing (YouTube tutorials) became the differentiator against TubeBuddy.

name: The Birdhouse
notes: The Birdhouse demonstrates the long_compound + skill_compound path. 5 years of losses were actually skill accumulation years. Key lesson: low-paying work is not wasted time if you are accumulating domain knowledge and transferable skills.

name: Draftss

pain_intensity.reason: Startups need consistent design without hiring

notes: Draftss demonstrates the long_compound + service_productization path. Converting non-standard freelance work into standardized, scalable delivery is one of the most reliable compound growth paths. 7-figure ARR with 124 PH votes shows strong community validation.

name: Tool Finder
notes: Tool Finder demonstrates the niche_compound + content_compound path. Every review article is a long-term asset that generates SEO traffic over years. 5-figure MRR with 75 PH votes shows strong validation.

name: Floga
notes: Floga demonstrates the pivot + audience_leverage path. Building an audience with physical products, then leveraging that trust for digital product launch. 81 PH votes and $10K MRR validate the approach.

name: Tallow
notes: Tallow demonstrates the passion_project + micro_acquisition path. Using exit funds and experience to build a slower but more sustainable product. $675/week with 1000 users shows it is a viable side project.

name: Loomin
success_reasons: Non-technical founder building SaaS with AI
notes: Loomin represents the trend of non-technical founders building SaaS with AI and no-code tools. 14 PH votes and community engagement show interest. Key insight: AI tools are lowering the technical barrier to entry for SaaS building.

name: Ferguson
target_user: Marketers, conversion rate optimizers, growth teams
notes: Ferguson demonstrates the productized_content + community_led_growth path. Offering free value (landing page diagnosis) as an acquisition hook is powerful. 63 comments show strong community interest in CRO.

name: GetRankOnMap
target_user: Local businesses, SEO agencies, franchise owners
notes: GetRankOnMap demonstrates the niche_positioning path. Not doing general SEO but specifically Google Maps rank tracking. Niche positioning reduces competition but also limits ceiling. 10 PH votes and 4 comments show early interest.

name: LiFast

clarity.reason: Clear efficiency value (10 hours to 10 minutes)

success_reasons: Clear efficiency metrics (10 hours to 10 minutes, 3x consultations)
notes: LiFast demonstrates the efficiency_leverage + build_in_public path. Developer tools need quantified value propositions. More efficient is less compelling than saves 10 hours or 3x client consultations. 27 PH votes and Build Board #3 ranking show strong market interest.
